questions.question: What does the international GLP standard regulate?
options: organization and carrying out of clinical trials of drugs
organization of preclinical studies of drugs on experimental animals and high-quality laboratory control of drugs
manufacture of high-quality drugs at pharmaceutical enterprises
organization of high-quality pharmacy (pharmaceutical) practice

options: organization and carrying out of clinical trials of drugs
organization of preclinical studies of drugs on experimental animals and high-quality laboratory control of drugs
manufacture of high-quality drugs at pharmaceutical enterprises
organization of high-quality pharmacy (pharmaceutical) practice

questions.question: What does the international GMP standard regulate?
options: organization and carrying out of clinical trials of drugs
organization of preclinical studies of drugs on experimental animals and high-quality laboratory control of drugs
manufacture of high-quality drugs at pharmaceutical enterprises
organization of high-quality pharmacy (pharmaceutical) practice

options: organization and carrying out of clinical trials of drugs
organization of preclinical studies of drugs on experimental animals and high-quality laboratory control of drugs
manufacture of high-quality drugs at pharmaceutical enterprises

questions.question: Choose proper definition of pharmacopoeial monograph for a medicinal product:
options: state drug standard
instructions for use of the drug
technical regulations for the production of the drug
price list from the manufacturer of the drug

questions.question: Choose proper groups of dosage forms on classification according to their state of aggregation:
options: gaseous
solid
semi-solid
semi-soft
soft
liquid

suppository
dragee
tablet
lozenge

questions.question: What of the following does not belong to semisolid dosage forms?
options: patch
caramel
suppository
gel
cream
paste

questions.question: Which ointment base is a product of petroleum refining?
options: lanolin
petroleum jelly
spermaceti
cocoa butter
wax

questions.question: What is proper content of powdery substances in pastes?
Which dosage form is solid at room temperature and melts at body temperature?
options: dragee
suppository
capsule
tablet
suspension

questions.question: Which solvent is most frequently used for the preparation of solutions?
options: purified (distilled) water
ethyl alcohol
olive oil
sunflower oil
glycerin

questions.question: Which dosage forms must be sterile and apyrogenec?
options: aromatic waters
medicinal enemas
liniments
infusions and decoctions
dosage forms for injections

questions.question: What dosage forms are aqueous extracts from plant raw materials?
options: tinctures
infusions
decoctions
extracts
aromatic waters

questions.question: Choose the correct continuation. Tablets are...:
options: solid undosed dosage form which looks as grains of spherical or other shape
solid dosed dosage form obtained by pressing powders and granules
dosed dosage form which is solid at room temperature and melting at body temperature
ready made dosage form obtained by special processing of plant raw materials

questions.question: Choose the correct continuation. Suppositories are...:
options: solid undosed dosage form which looks as grains of spherical or other shape
solid dosed dosage form obtained by pressing powders and granules
dosed dosage form which is solid at room temperature and melting at body temperature
ready made dosage form obtained by special processing of plant raw materials

questions.question: What are proper types of suppositories?
options: oral
rectal
vaginal
ophthalmic

questions.question: What is Pharmacopoeia?
options: a part of general pharmacology about routes of drug administration, absorption, distribution in the body, biotransformation and routes of elimination
a part of general pharmacology about mechanisms of drug action and drug effects
a collection of state national standards and requirements for medicinal substances, medicinal raw materials and drugs
official authority for drug quality control
a science of medicinal raw materials

questions.question: What is pharmacodynamics?
options: a collection of state national standards and requirements for medicinal substances, medicinal raw materials and drugs
a part of general pharmacology about mechanisms of drug action and drug effects
a part of general pharmacology about routes of drug administration, absorption, distribution in the body, biotransformation and routes of elimination a science of medicinal raw materials
a science of methods of drug research

questions.question: What is pharmacokinetics?
options: a part of general pharmacology about drug absorption, distribution in the body, biotransformation and routes of excretion
a science of methods of drug research
a science of medicinal raw materials
a part of general pharmacology about mechanisms of drug action and their effects
a collection of state national standards and requirements for medicinal substances, medicinal raw materials and drugs

questions.question: What is elimination?
options: accumulation of a medicinal substance in blood plasma
ability of a drug to pass through biological barriers
interaction of a drug with tissue receptors
removal of an active form of a medicinal substance out of the body due to biotransformation and excretion
distribution of a medicinal substance in biological liquids of the body

questions.question: What is acquired tolerance?
options: increase in the body sensitivity to medicinal substance
insuperable attraction to repeat dose of a drug
perverted reaction of the body on drug introduction
gradual weakening of therapeutic action of a drug
storage of a drug in certain tissues of the body

questions.question: What is synergism?
options: perverted reaction of the body to drug administration
unidirectional action of medicinal substances what results in reinforcement of their final effect
opposite action of medicinal substances what results in weakening of their final effect
accumulation of a drug in tissues of the body

questions.question: What is antagonism?
options: unidirectional action of medicinal substances what results in reinforcement of their final effect
opposite action of medicinal substances what results in weakening of their final effect
gradual weakening of a therapeutic drug effect
impossibility of drug administration in certain dosage form
a delay of drug elimination

questions.question: What is idiosyncrasy?
options: rapid development of tolerance to a medicinal substance
acute exacerbation of a disease after drug withdrawal
perverted rapid reaction of the body on first introduction of a drug
a reaction of the body due to exceeding the therapeutic dose of a drug
negative influence of a medicinal substance on a fetus

questions.question: What is agonist (mimetic)?
options: medicinal substance which blocks interaction of endogenous ligands with receptors
medicinal substance which acts on receptors similar to endogenous ligands
medicinal substance which binds to plasma albumins and circulate in the blood for a long time
drug which is rapidly eliminated from the body
inducer of microsomal liver enzymes

questions.question: What does chronopharmacology study?
options: dependence of pharmacological effects on dose and concentration of a drug
routes of metabolic transformation of medicinal substances
routes of drug excretion
dependence of drug bioavailability on physical and chemical properties of medicinal substances
dependence of pharmacological effects on biological rhythms of the body

questions.question: Choose proper mechanisms of drug absorption from the g.i.t.:
options: passive diffusion
facilitated diffusion
active transport
inverse secretion
all answers are correct

questions.question: Choose drugs with more intensive absorption from the g.i.t.:
options: non-polar (not ionized)
polar (ionized)

questions.question: Choose proper formula of therapeutic index:
options: LD/ED
ED/LD
mass of medicinal substance (g) /mass of a body (kg)

questions.question: What does therapeutic index characterize?
options: strength of pharmacological effect
duration of drug effect
drug safety
possibility of allergic reaction

questions.question: What is a definition of bioavailability?
options: route of drug administration
amount of a drug which reached the systemic circulation as compared to initial dose
single dose of a drug
course dose of a drug

questions.question: What is a percentage of bioavailability of a drug at oral administration?
options: less than 100 %
more than 100 %

questions.question: Choose proper feature of permeability of blood-brain barrier (BBB):
options: BBB is permeable for all medicinal substances
BBB is impermeable for all medicinal substances
BBB is selectively permeable for different medicinal substances

questions.question: Choose proper definition of biotransformation of a medicinal substance:
options: biochemical changes of a medicinal substance in the body
accumulation of a medicinal substance in tissues
plasma protein binding
elimination of drugs and their metabolites out of the body

questions.question: What is excretion?
options: biochemical changes of a medicinal substance in the body
accumulation of a medicinal substance in tissues
plasma protein binding
elimination of a drug and its metabolites out of the body

questions.question: Choose proper pathways of drug biotransformation in the body (types of reactions):
options: not synthetic (metabolic transformation)
synthetic (conjugation)
anabolic
catalytic

questions.question: Which of the following are indices of elimination?
options: a period of half-elimination (half-life)
blood sedimentation rate
clearance
LD/ED

questions.question: What is a period of half-elimination (half-life)?
options: time during which therapeutic effect decreases on 50%
time during which drug concentration in blood plasma decreases on 50%
time during which a drug is registered in blood plasma

questions.question: What is systemic action of a drug?
options: its action after absorption into the blood
its action at the place of application
its action on sensory receptors with development of reflex response in internal organs

questions.question: What is local action of a drug?
options: its action after absorption into the blood
its action at the place of application
its action on sensory receptors with the development of reflex response in internal organs

questions.question: What is reflex action of drug?
options: its action after absorption into the blood
its action at the place of application
its action on sensory receptors with the development of reflex response in internal organs

questions.question: What effects of drugs are named as main effects?
options: effects which provide therapeutic action of a drug, relieve symptoms of disease and improve quality of life
all effects of therapeutic dose
only those effects of minimal dose of a drug

questions.question: Which kind of pharmacotherapy is called as etiotropic (causal) one?
options: directed towards elimination of particular symptoms of disease
directed towards weakening of pathogenetic mechanisms of disease
directed towards elimination of cause of disease

questions.question: What medicinal substances are called as enzyme inducers?
options: drugs which increase synthesis of enzymes and their activity
drugs which decrease activity of enzymes
drugs used for replacement therapy in enzyme deficiency

questions.question: What medicinal substances are called as enzyme inhibitors?
options: drugs which increase synthesis of enzymes
drugs which decrease activity of enzymes
drugs used for replacement therapy of enzyme deficiency

questions.question: What is a prodrug?
options: a substance which is metabolized in the body and losses pharmacological activity
a substance which is metabolized in the body and gets pharmacological activity
a drug which does not undergo metabolic transformation in the body

questions.question: What is cumulation?
options: accumulation (storage) of a drug in the body
hypersensitivity of the body to a drug
process of drug metabolism

questions.question: Choose proper kinds of drug cumulation:
options: physiological
pathological
material
functional

questions.question: Which reactions can occur at sudden discontinuation of a drug after long-term use?
options: allergic ones
withdrawal syndrome
rebound syndrome
idiosyncrasy

questions.question: Which of the following are immuno-alergic adverse effects (type B)?
options: anaphylactic shock
carcenogenic effect
urticaria
drug dependence
dyspepsia due to aspirin

questions.question: Which of the following adverse effects depend on pharmacodynamics (type A)?
options: anaphylactic shock
carcenogenic effect
urticaria
drug dependence
dry mouth due to atropine

questions.question: Choose adverse effects due to long-term pharmacotherapy (type C):
options: anaphylactic shock
cancerogenic effect
urticaria
drug dependence
dyspepsia due to aspirin

questions.question: Choose delayed adverse effects (type D):
options: anaphylactic shock
carcenogenic effect
urticaria
drug dependence
dyspepsia due to application of aspirin

questions.question: What is embryotoxic effect?
options: toxic action of a drug on an embryo (during the first 3 weeks of pregnancy) which result in death of the embryo in majority cases
toxic action of a drug on the fetus (from 3d to 10-12th week of pregnancy) what affects organogenesis of the fetus
toxic action of a drug on the fetus in fetal period (after 3-4 months of pregnancy)
any toxic action of a drug during pregnancy

questions.question: What is teratogenicity?
options: toxic action of a drug on an embryo (during the first 3 weeks of pregnancy) which result in death of the embryo in majority cases
toxic action of a drug on the fetus (from 3d to 10-12th week of pregnancy) what affects organogenesis of the fetus
toxic action of a drug on the fetus in fetal period (after 3-4 months of pregnancy)
any toxic action of a drug during pregnancy

questions.question: What is fetotoxic drug action?
options: toxic action of a drug on an embryo (during the first 3 weeks of pregnancy) which result in death of the embryo in majority cases
toxic action of a drug on the fetus (from 3d to 10-12th week of pregnancy) what affects organogenesis of the fetus
toxic action of a drug on the fetus in fetal period (after 3-4 months of pregnancy)
any toxic action of a drug during pregnancy

questions.question: What is minimum (threshold) therapeutic dose?
options: amount of a drug which cause minimal therapeutic effect
amount of a drug which cause marked therapeutic effect in the majority of patients
amount of a drug which cause maximal therapeutic effect without toxic effects
amount of a drug for single administration
dose of a drug for a day
amount of a drug for a whole course of treatment

questions.question: An average therapeutic dose is:
options: amount of a drug which cause minimal therapeutic effect
amount of a drug which cause marked therapeutic effect in the majority of patients
amount of a drug which cause maximal therapeutic effect without toxic effects
amount of a drug for single administration
dose of a drug for a day
amount of a drug for a whole course of treatment

questions.question: What is maximum therapeutic dose?
options: amount of a drug which cause minimal therapeutic effect
amount of a drug which cause marked therapeutic effect in the majority of patients
amount of a drug which cause maximal therapeutic effect without toxic effects
amount of a drug for single administration
dose of a drug for a day
amount of a drug for a whole course of treatment

questions.question: What is single dose?
options: amount of a drug which cause minimal therapeutic effect
amount of a drug which cause marked therapeutic effect in the majority of patients
amount of a drug which cause maximal therapeutic effect without toxic effects
amount of a drug for single administration
dose of a drug for a day
amount of a drug for a whole course of treatment

questions.question: What is daily dose?
options: amount of a drug which cause minimal therapeutic effect
amount of a drug which cause marked therapeutic effect in the majority of patients
amount of a drug which cause maximal therapeutic effect without toxic effects
amount of a drug for single administration
dose of a drug for a day
amount of a drug for a whole course of treatment

questions.question: What is course dose?
options: amount of a drug which cause minimum therapeutic effect
amount of a drug which cause marked therapeutic effect in the majority of patients
amount of a drug which cause maximum therapeutic effect without toxic effects
amount of a drug for single administration
dose of a drug for a day
amount of a drug for a whole course of treatment

questions.question: What is a purpose of knock-out (loading) dose of a drug?
options: to prevent adverse effects
to produce therapeutic concentration of a drug in the body quickly
to prolong pharmacological effect

questions.question: What doses are called as toxic ones?
options: dose which exceeds average therapeutic dose
dose which exceeds maximum therapeutic dose
dose which causes death

questions.question: Choose correct statements:
options: pharmacological effect depends on dose of a drug
pharmacological effect does not depend on dose of a drug
pharmacological effect depends on drug concentration
pharmacological effect does not depend on drug concentration

questions.question: What factors influence drug action in the body?
options: physicochemical properties of a drug
specific features of the body
political factors
social factors
climatic conditions
